name: Anise Pine-Nut Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Using an electric mixer, cream together the butter, shortening, and sugars until the mixture is light and fluffy. Beat in the vanilla.
2. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and baking powder. Gradually add this dry mixture to the butter mixture.
3. Finely grind 1/2 cup of cooled pine nuts with aniseed using a food processor or spice grinder. Add this mixture to the dough and blend well.
4. Drop teaspoons of the dough about 2 inches apart onto greased baking sheets, topping each cookie with several remaining pine nuts.
5. Bake in a preheated oven at 325°F for 18 to 20 minutes until golden brown.
6. Remove the baking sheets from the heat and let the cookies cool on the sheets for 2 minutes. Carefully transfer the cookies (they are delicate) to wire racks.
7. Allow the cookies to cool completely before storing them in airtight containers.
